name: Embalse La Viñuela – circular desde Periana
region_intro: Touring cycling around Periana offers diverse and picturesque landscapes within the Axarquía region of Málaga, Andalusia. The area features stunning mountainous terrain, including the Sierras Alhama and Gallo-Vilo, with panoramic views of Lake Viñuela and the Mediterranean Sea. Extensive olive groves and fruit orchards characterize the rural areas, while the Guaro River contributes to the scenic beauty. Best touring cycling routes around Periana The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse La Viñuela – circular desde Periana, a 17.0 miles (27.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 8 minutes to complete. This route offers expansive views of Lake Viñuela and the surrounding mountains. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Monument to Peace – Torre del Mar Beach loop from Moya, a difficult 58.6 miles (94.4 km) path. This longer route connects inland landscapes with coastal views towards Torre del Mar Beach. Local touring cyclists also love the Venta de Alfarnate & Museum – Puerto del Sol (1085 m) loop from Las Mayorales, a 28.8 miles (46.3 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and the Puerto del Sol mountain pass, often completed in about 3 hours 26 minutes. Touring cycling around Periana is defined by mountainous backdrops, verdant olive groves, and the tranquil Lake Viñuela. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes feature significant elevation gain. The routes in Periana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Periana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Periana?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes available around Periana, offering a wide variety of options for different preferences and fitness levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 700 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Periana?
A: Touring cycling routes in Periana feature diverse and picturesque landscapes. You'll encounter stunning mountainous terrain, including the Sierras Alhama and Gallo-Vilo, with significant elevation gains. The region is also characterized by extensive olive groves and fruit orchards, offering charming rural sections. Many routes combine paved and unpaved surfaces.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Periana?
A: While many routes in Periana feature significant elevation gain and are rated as difficult, there are 5 moderate routes available. These routes offer a less strenuous experience while still allowing you to enjoy the beautiful scenery.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see while cycling in Periana?
A: Periana's touring cycling routes offer access to several natural and historical highlights. You can cycle past the ancient Moorish thermal baths at &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5061410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños de Vilo Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy views of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3256393" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Maroma Massif&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/388446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hundred‑Spout Fountain&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its extensive olive groves and the tranquil Lake Viñuela.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Periana?
A: Yes, Periana offers several circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981469026/embalse-la-vinuela-circular-desde-periana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse La Viñuela – circular desde Periana&lt;/a&gt;, which provides expansive views of Lake Viñuela and the surrounding mountains. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14594013" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Venta de Alfarnate & Museum – Puerto del Sol (1085 m) loop from Las Mayorales&lt;/a&gt;, leading through mountainous terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Periana?
A: The Axarquía region, where Periana is located, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly ideal, with milder temperatures perfect for tackling the area's varied terrain. Summers can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winters are generally mild but can bring cooler temperatures to the mountainous areas.

Q: Are there options for longer, more challenging touring cycling routes in the Periana area?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31941084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to Peace – Torre del Mar Beach loop from Moya&lt;/a&gt; offer a difficult 58.6-mile (94.4 km) path with over 2,000 meters of elevation gain, connecting inland landscapes with coastal views. Many of the 45 difficult routes available provide significant distances and ascents.

Q: Can I find parking easily near the starting points of touring cycling routes in Periana?
A: Periana is a small village, and parking is generally available within or on the outskirts of the village, making it convenient to start many routes directly from there. For routes starting from other nearby towns or specific trailheads, local parking options are usually indicated on komoot route details.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Periana?
A: The touring cycling routes around Periana are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the stunning mountainous backdrops, verdant olive groves, and the tranquil Lake Viñuela, highlighting the diverse and picturesque landscapes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Periana?
A: Given the mountainous terrain and significant elevation gains characteristic of Periana, most touring cycling routes are rated as moderate to difficult. While there are no specifically designated 'easy' routes, families with older children or those comfortable with some climbing might enjoy shorter sections of the moderate routes, particularly those around Lake Viñuela, which offer scenic views without extreme technical difficulty.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that explore the region's olive groves?
A: Yes, cycling through the extensive olive groves is a quintessential Periana experience. The region is famous for its verdial olive oil, and many routes, such as those that are part of the 'Route of the Olive Oil and Mountains,' traverse these charming rural landscapes. You'll find yourself cycling past ancient olive trees and fruit orchards, particularly peach and quince.

Q: Where can I find refreshments or places to stop along the touring cycling routes?
A: Periana and the surrounding villages offer various cafes, restaurants, and small shops where you can stop for refreshments. Many routes pass through or near these villages, providing opportunities to refuel and experience local hospitality. It's always a good idea to check the route details on komoot for specific points of interest or towns along your chosen path.
